frame.annual_federal_proxy: +$1,519/yr for Mexico <HS recent-surge adults (SIPP donor cells; payroll > transfers)
frame.local_shock_ledger: school enrollment + per-pupil, shelter/emergency housing, Medicaid/state health — episodic and jurisdictional, not in lifetime NPV scalars
frame.tension: Federal-positive cell proxies can coexist with state/local net-negative surge ledgers (CBO 61256) and asylum shelter shocks (Gould w33655)

extraction_notes.butchart_w6067: Local PDF is scanned (CVISION 1997); pdftotext unusable. Quantitative claims from NBER w6067 non-technical summary [SOURCE: https://www.nber.org/papers/w6067].
extraction_notes.borjas_w6175: Local PDF is scanned; pdftotext unusable. Claims from NBER/SSRN abstract and published RLE chapter summary [SOURCE: https://ssrn.com/abstract=225933].

parameter_claims.notes: Direct revenues +$10.1B vs spending +$19.3B; 0.3% of state-local spending net of federal grants-in-aid.

parameter_claims.population: surge population including nonbudgetary crowding costs
parameter_claims.notes: Potential revenues +$18.8B, spending +$28.6B. Treats classroom/transport crowding as fiscal pressure even when spending unchanged — framing choice.
550,000 surge children = 1.1% of public school students; potential K-12 cost band up to $9.4B.
Episodic local shock line item; not in federal lifetime NPV studies.
43% national sheltered rise (149k people); 75% concentrated in NYC, Chicago, MA, Denver.
Within <200% poverty, immigrants participate safety net at lower rates than natives for most programs; school lunch exception.
8.4% foreign-born households received 10.1% of assistance-recipient households; welfare participation 9.1% vs natives +1.7pp gap by 1990.
≈4.2% of flow; benefit elasticity 1.3. Causal treatment effect on destination choice, not selection into low skill.

parameter_claims.population: EUR free-migration vs non-EUR restricted migration, 1990-2000
parameter_claims.notes: Negative generosity→skill under free migration (magnet); positive under policy-controlled migration (fiscal burden/selectivity). Papers often pool regimes.

parameter_claims.population: Presidio County TX (+10pp low-skilled immigrant share)
parameter_claims.notes: Monterey CA +14% with opposite skill mix; national net ≈0.3%/yr because low- and high-skilled offset.

parameter_claims.population: US immigrants overall vs by education
parameter_claims.notes: Aggregate federal positive (CBO 2024 cited); state/local cost mainly K-12 for US-born children of immigrants — assigns descendant costs to immigrant row.
Local distributional offset: bottom-decile mobility gain vs 8th-decile college completion −3.5pp; not a fiscal ledger item.
Native-born men 2.16% (1990) vs 1.35% (1980). Crime/incarceration is local cost channel but immigrants lower per-capita — disconfirms naive fiscal-crime link.
~80% of ethnic welfare participation gap transmitted parent→child. Selection vs treatment confound for lifetime NPV.
Immigrants consume less housing per equivalent wage; remittance motive reduces local cost-of-living pass-through — offsets naive rent-demand local shock.
Add-on local cost not in NAS lifetime ledger; immigrant urban concentration (FRBSF) maps to this margin.

generators.name: Federal-positive / local-negative ledger split
generators.prompt: For each fiscal immigration study, tag every cost and revenue line as FEDERAL vs STATE/LOCAL vs EPISODIC-SHOCK (shelter, border). Where does the paper silently aggregate jurisdictions? Cross-walk to our SIPP annual federal proxy (+$1,519/yr Mexico <HS) vs CBO-style local shock ledger (school, shelter, Medicaid). List papers that report net positive lifetime NPV but omit local surge lines.
generators.retrodiction: Would have surfaced CBO 61256 $9.2B direct net cost, Gould shelter 60% of homelessness rise, Orrenius 2025 federal-positive/state-local-negative split, and our scenario-composition frame tension before treating SIPP cells as scalar verdict.
generators.negative_space: Lifetime NPV and generational accounting collapse jurisdiction and time horizon — hiding episodic shelter shocks and balanced-budget state constraints.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Federal surplus on immigrant row implies locality solvent
Annual SIPP proxy and lifetime NPV are interchangeable signs
Crowding costs don't count unless budget line moves

generators.name: Welfare magnet: selection vs treatment vs policy regime
generators.prompt: Map each welfare-migration paper to (a) migrant self-selection (treatment on movers), (b) host policy filter (treatment on admissions), (c) free vs restricted mobility regime. Does generosity affect skill mix (Razin-Wahba) or flow level (Landais Denmark DiD)? Flag studies that interpret correlation as magnet without regime dummies.
generators.retrodiction: Would have found Razin-Wahba sign flip, Landais 5k/yr causal reduction and elasticity 1.3, Bitler-Hoynes post-PRWORA immigrant lower participation within poor households, Borjas w4872 assimilation-into-welfare cohort effects.
generators.negative_space: US interstate magnet literature often ignores that PRWORA made immigration policy federalism — treatment is policy+selection entangled.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Higher immigrant welfare use proves magnet caused inflow
Benefit cuts only affect intensive margin not location choice
Denmark quasi-experiment generalizes to US unauthorized surge

generators.name: Crime/incarceration: fiscal line item or labor spillover?
generators.prompt: Separate incarceration as (1) direct state/local budget line (CBO incarceration spending) from (2) labor-market/competition spillover (Borjas-Grogger-Hanson) from (3) compositional base-rate artifact (Butcher-Piehl native 2.16% vs immigrant 0.7%). Which lifetime fiscal models include any of these channels?
generators.retrodiction: Would have flagged Butcher w6067 low immigrant incarceration vs rising native rates, CBO surge prison share (4% state, 7% pop), and brainstorm note that NAS ledger omits crime channel.
generators.negative_space: Advocacy frames swap fiscal incarceration costs for crime rates; empirical lit shows immigrants lower institutionalization — channel is distributional not average fiscal drain.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Immigration raises incarceration spend proportionally to stock
Comparing immigrants to all natives is race-neutral
Crime costs belong in lifetime NPV if not in annual SIPP proxy

generators.name: Asylum/shelter episodic shock outside lifetime NPV
generators.prompt: Identify spending that is (a) emergency/episodic, (b) city-concentrated, (c) absent from NAS/CBO federal lifetime tables. Quantify shelter PIT/HUD linkage (Gould) vs K-12 entitlement (CBO Plyler) vs Medicaid federal-state match. Would discounting lifetime NPV miss 2022-2024 shelter spike?
generators.retrodiction: Would have surfaced Gould 60% of 43% sheltered rise, CBO $3.3B shelter in 4 states, NYC +77,352 sheltered 2022-2024, and mismatch with smooth lifetime NPV curves.
generators.negative_space: Lifetime fiscal synthesis uses steady-state education/transfer paths — asylum parole surges are state-local balance-sheet events.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Emergency shelter is temporary so NPV≈0
Federal parole policy externalizes costs to destination cities
HUD PIT counts equal budget appropriations
generators.theory_tested: shelter_spending_2023 / k12_spending_2023 ratio for surge cohort

generators.name: County fiscal capacity heterogeneity
generators.prompt: Where do national-average fiscal estimates hide county-level capacity constraints? Use BoC county IV design: low-skilled inflow → per-capita revenue down, spending down (Presidio −15%) vs high-skilled uplift (Monterey +14%). Cross to our PUMA/stage5 school spend weights and FRBSF urban concentration.
generators.retrodiction: Would have found BoC 0.3%/yr national offset masking ±15% county swings, FRBSF urban/remittance concentration, VMT $0.218/urban-mile congestion add-on, CBO 56% surge in 6 states.
generators.negative_space: Scalar 'Mexico NPV' erases geography — same immigrant type can expand or contract local public goods depending on county skill mix and transfer insurance.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: National NAS cell applies to border county
Intergovernmental transfers fully insure local fiscal shocks
Urban settlement implies higher per-capita local costs unconditionally

generators.name: Descendant and mobility offsets on local burden
generators.prompt: Track who bears local costs vs who captures fiscal upside: Orrenius assigns K-12 cost to US-born children; Akers-Boustan shows bottom-decile native HS completion +2.2pp per 10pp inflow; Blau-Kahn decomposes inequality not net fiscal position. Does immigrant-headed household federal proxy include citizen-child benefits without citizen-child future taxes?
generators.retrodiction: Would have connected Orrenius descendant-cost framing, Akers-Boustan decile-heterogeneous mobility, Blau-Kahn compositional inequality, Bitler-Hoynes 90% immigrant households contain native-born members.
generators.negative_space: Household-weighted federal proxy treats citizen children as immigrant fiscal property in both costs and benefits.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: School cost of US-born child belongs in immigrant lifetime NPV
Bottom-decile mobility gains net out top-decile losses in fiscal ledger

theories_tested.theory: Federal-positive annual proxy and local-negative shock ledger coexist for Mexico <HS surge cells
theories_tested.prediction: sign(avg_federal_net_proxy) > 0 while sign(state_local_net_2023) < 0 for same origin-education bucket
theories_tested.duckdb_test: JOIN origin_fiscal_scenario_2023 (avg_federal_net) WITH state_stage5_context_2023 + receiver_city_migrant_costs ON origin/state; compare federal_proxy to shelter+k12 shock lines
theories_tested.falsifier: Local net ledger turns positive after scaling school/shelter to per-adult and netting descendant payroll

theories_tested.theory: Welfare magnet operates on location/treatment more than skill selection in restricted regimes
theories_tested.prediction: Benefit cuts reduce inflows (Landais elasticity ~1.3) without raising immigrant education percentile
theories_tested.duckdb_test: No direct test in warehouse — register Landais/Denmark as external prior; US test uses state TANF/SNAP variation × ACS origin flows
theories_tested.falsifier: Post-PRWORA immigrant inflows rose to high-benefit states with no DiD break

theories_tested.theory: Asylum shelter shock is episodic and not captured in smooth lifetime NPV
theories_tested.prediction: shelter_spending_2022_2024 >> discounted NPV increment from annual federal proxy for same cohort
theories_tested.duckdb_test: Compare receiver_city_migrant_costs annual shelter to origin_fiscal_scenario avg_federal_net * weighted_adults for surge origins
theories_tested.falsifier: Shelter costs revert to zero in 2025+ and NPV amortization matches cumulative shelter

theories_tested.theory: County fiscal capacity mediates local burden more than national average NAS cell
theories_tested.prediction: High low_skill_share counties show per_pupil_spend pressure (stage5) even when national federal proxy positive
theories_tested.duckdb_test: JOIN origin_puma_household_stage5_context_2023 WITH state_el_lep_2018 and area_wtd_current_spend_per_pupil; stratify by low_skill_share tercile
theories_tested.falsifier: Per-pupil spend and EL counts uncorrelated with immigrant share after RPP adjustment

theories_tested.theory: Immigrant incarceration channel is fiscal rounding error not primary local cost driver
theories_tested.prediction: CBO incarceration spend increment << K-12 + shelter for surge population
theories_tested.duckdb_test: Extract incarceration line from CBO 61256 spending table vs education+shelter subtotals
theories_tested.falsifier: Incarceration spend >10% of direct surge spending increase
